user_message: Review the current code changes (staged, unstaged, and untracked files) and provide prioritized findings.
assistant_summary: {"findings":[{"title":"[P2] Avoid mixing current session with selection turn in CLI outcomes","body":"When `ec decision outcome` is used with `--selection-id` during a newly started session that has no turns yet, `detect_current_context()` returns `(current_session, None)`. Passing those values through separately lets `record_decision_outcome()` keep the new `session_id` but backfill `turn_id` from the old selection, so the stored turn belongs to a different session than the stored session. That produces inconsistent telemetry rows and misattributes later session/turn-based analysis.","confidence_score":0.92,"priority":2,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/home/teslamint/Workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/cli/decisions_cmds.py","line_range":{"start":230,"end":238}}},{"title":"[P2] Avoid mixing current session with selection turn in MCP outcomes","body":"The MCP wrapper has the same partial-context issue as the CLI path: if there is an active session but no current turn, `session_id=session_id or current_session_id` and `turn_id=turn_id or current_turn_id` pass only half of the current context into `record_decision_outcome()`. With a `selection_id` from an earlier retrieval, the core helper then fills `turn_id` from that selection, yielding an outcome row whose `session_id` and `turn_id` refer to different sessions.","confidence_score":0.94,"priority":2,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/home/teslamint/Workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/mcp/tools/decisions.py","line_range":{"start":91,"end":99}}}],"overall_correctness":"patch is incorrect","overall_explanation":"The new decision-outcome feature is mostly implemented correctly, but both entry points can persist inconsistent session/turn references for selection-based outcomes. That creates incorrect telemetry in a realistic workflow, so the patch should not be considered fully correct yet.","overall_confidence_score":0.9}
